doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document grossly misrepresents Illinois eviction law—a 5-day notice is merely a prerequisite to filing a court eviction action, not self-executing authority to force vacating—and HUD would never issue guidance stripping tenants of their
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: This document is deeply misleading and legally false — Illinois's 5-day notice is a prerequisite to filing an eviction lawsuit, not itself authority to force a tenant to vacate without court proceedings; tenants absolutely have the right to remain and contest eviction in court; HUD would never issue guidance telling tenants they have no right to a hearing, and the document number, co-issuance format, and phone number do not correspond to real HUD publications.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: The document grossly misrepresents Illinois eviction law—a 5-day notice is merely a prerequisite to filing a court eviction action, not self-executing authority to force vacating—and HUD would never issue guidance stripping tenants of their right to a court hearing.
